enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Identifying Adjectives and Adverbs
Read the sentences below. Identify the adjective(s) and adverb(s) in each sentence. Write 'Adj' for adjective and 'Adv' for adverb above each word you identify.

questions.texte: The climate change is a very serious problem for our planet.
She speaks English fluently, but her brother speaks slowly.
Endangered species often live in extremely fragile ecosystems.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Complete the Table
Fill in the missing comparative and superlative forms of the following adjectives.

Exercise 3: Rewrite Sentences
Rewrite the sentences by placing the adverb of frequency in the correct position.

questions.reponse: She is always late for school.
We usually eat a balanced diet.

questions.texte: He has seen a doctor for his headache. (never)
questions.reponse: He has never seen a doctor for his headache.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Create Comparisons
Using the prompts, write complete sentences using comparative or superlative forms.

questions.reponse: A tornado is more dangerous than a flood.
Smoking is one of the worst habits for the lungs.

questions.texte: She / speak French / fluent / her brother
questions.reponse: She speaks French more fluently than her brother.

intitule: Real-Life Problem Solving: Healthy Lifestyle Advice

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Health Campaign Poster
Your school is organizing a health awareness campaign. You are asked to write three short slogans or pieces of advice for teenagers about healthy eating and exercise. In each sentence, you must use at least one comparative adjective, one superlative adjective, and one adverb of frequency.
images: A school health awareness poster with fruits, vegetables, and children exercising

questions.texte: Write your first slogan about eating habits.
questions.reponse: Example: Eating fresh vegetables is healthier than eating fast food, and you should always choose a balanced diet for the strongest body.

questions.texte: Write your second slogan about physical activity.
questions.reponse: Example: Regular exercise is more important than you think; the most active students usually have more energy and focus in class.

questions.texte: Write your third slogan about avoiding harmful substances.
questions.reponse: Example: Smoking damages your lungs faster than anything else; the wisest teenagers never start this dangerous habit.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Find and Correct Mistakes
Each sentence contains one mistake related to adjectives or adverbs. Identify the mistake and rewrite the sentence correctly.

questions.texte: This is the more delicious meal I have ever eaten.
questions.reponse: This is the most delicious meal I have ever eaten.

questions.texte: She runs very quick to catch the bus.
questions.reponse: She runs very quickly to catch the bus.

questions.texte: Of all the students, he did the test more carefully than everyone.
questions.reponse: Of all the students, he did the test the most carefully. OR He did the test more carefully than everyone else.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 7: Paragraph Writing
Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) describing your neighborhood or community. You must include:

  • At least 3 comparative adjectives
  • At least 2 superlative adjectives
  • At least 2 adverbs of frequency
  • At least 2 adverbs of manner
images: A peaceful residential neighborhood in Cameroon with houses, trees, and people walking

questions.reponse: Example: My neighborhood is quieter than the city center, and it is one of the safest places in our town. People usually greet each other politely in the morning. The local market is cheaper than the supermarket, but the supermarket has the freshest vegetables. Children often play carefully near the main road. Living here is more peaceful than living in a crowded area, and my neighbors are the friendliest people I know.
